name: Szechuan Dry-Braised Prawns
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Dry sherry (or sake wine for better flavor)
recipeInstructions:
1. Shell and devein the shrimp, leaving tails attached. Rinse and pat dry.
2. Combine MARINADE ingredients with shrimp in the order listed. Let stand for 10 minutes.
3. Oil blanch: Set wok over high heat for about 1 minute. Add 3 tablespoons oil and heat to 300°F (150°C). Add shrimp, stirring gently to separate. Blanch until pink, then remove and drain.
4. To braise: Remove all but 3 tablespoons oil from the wok. Add onion and brown slightly; stir in remaining spices and cook until fragrant. Add seasonings and shrimp, then braise over high heat until sauce is reduced. Stir in green onion and sesame oil.
5. Transfer to a platter to serve.
NOTES:
1. Adjust the chili paste according to your own taste.
2. Substitute 2 lobster tails for meat to make dry-braised lobster.
3. Using smaller shrimp and less sauce makes this dish suitable for an appetizer. Enjoy!
